revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

revision.comment: Programmatically generating so that if this gets imported to wikipedia as a draft, the bot will attribute it to Immanuelle and send them g6 warnings
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Suwa_Shrine_(Sapporo)]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

revision.comment: 長野県からの移住者が作った神社
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=諏訪神社 |画像=[[File:Suwa Shrine of Sapporo.JPG|280px|諏訪神社]] | 緯度度 =43 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 33.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 =141 |経度分 =21 | 経度秒 =14.6 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |所在地=[[北海道]][[札幌市]][[東区 (札幌市)|東区]]北12条東1丁目1番10号 |社格=村社 |祭神=[[建御名方神|建御名方命]]・[[八坂刀売神|八坂刀売命]] |創建=[[1882年]]（[[明治]]15年）3月12日 |本殿= |例祭=9月12日 }} '''諏訪神社'''（すわじんじゃ）は[[北海道]][[札幌市]][[東区 (札幌市)|東区]]北12条東1丁目1番10号にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は村社。
祭神は[[建御名方神|建御名方命（たけみなかたのみこと）]]と[[八坂刀売神|八坂刀売命（やさかとめのみこと）]]。
境内の[[ヤチダモ]]と[[ハルニレ]]の8本の樹木が、札幌市によって保存樹に指定されている<ref>[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html 札幌市東区 - 文化財など]</ref>。
== 歴史 == *[[1878年]]（[[明治]]11年）、[[信濃国]]（[[長野県]]）から約30名が当地に移住する<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html 北海道神社庁 - 諏訪神社]</ref>。 *[[1882年]]（明治15年）3月12日、信濃国一の宮・官幣大社[[諏訪神社]]より分霊を勧請して奉る<ref name="jinjacho" />。 *[[1898年]]（明治31年）2月14日、内務省に出願し無格社となる<ref name="jinjacho" />。 *[[1934年]]（[[昭和]]9年）4月19日、村社に列格。また同年には所在地が[[札幌村]]から札幌市に編入され、これにあわせて社殿と社務所が造営される<ref name="jinjacho" />。 *[[1945年]]（昭和20年）、郷社への昇格を申請するも<ref name="jinjacho" />、実現せず。 *[[1966年]]（昭和41年）、新社殿造営<ref name="jinjacho" />。 *[[1982年]]（昭和57年）、鎮座百年祭<ref name="jinjacho" />。 *[[1992年]]（[[平成]]4年）、鎮座百十年祭。社殿の大屋根葺き替え<ref name="jinjacho" />。 *[[2002年]]（平成14年）、鎮座百二十年祭<ref name="jinjacho" />。 *[[2012年]]（平成24年）、鎮座百三十年祭<ref name="jinjacho" />。
== 脚注 == <references /> == 外部リンク == *[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html 北海道神社庁 - 諏訪神社]
{{デフォルトソート:すわしんしや}} [[Category:札幌市の神社]] [[Category:札幌市東区の歴史]] [[Category:北海道の旧村社]]

{{神社 |名称=諏訪神社 |画像=[[File:Suwa Shrine of Sapporo.JPG|280px|諏訪神社]] | 緯度度 =43 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 33.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 =141 |経度分 =21 | 経度秒 =14.6 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |所在地=[[北海道]][[札幌市]][[東区 (札幌市)|東区]]北12条東1丁目1番10号 |社格=村社 |祭神=[[建御名方神|建御名方命]]・[[八坂刀売神|八坂刀売命]] |創建=[[1882年]]（[[明治]]15年）3月12日 |本殿= |例祭=9月12日 }} '''諏訪神社'''（すわじんじゃ）は[[北海道]][[札幌市]][[東区 (札幌市)|東区]]北12条東1丁目1番10号にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は村社。
祭神は[[建御名方神|建御名方命（たけみなかたのみこと）]]と[[八坂刀売神|八坂刀売命（やさかとめのみこと）]]。
境内の[[ヤチダモ]]と[[ハルニレ]]の8本の樹木が、札幌市によって保存樹に指定されている<ref>[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html 札幌市東区 - 文化財など]</ref>。

{{神社 |名称=諏訪神社 |画像=[[File:Suwa Shrine of Sapporo.JPG|280px|諏訪神社]] | 緯度度 =43 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 33.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 =141 |経度分 =21 | 経度秒 =14.6 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Hokkaido |所在地=[[北海道]][[札幌市]][[東区 (札幌市)|東区]]北12条東1丁目1番10号 |社格=村社 |祭神=[[建御名方神|建御名方命]]・[[八坂刀売神|八坂刀売命]] |創建=[[1882年]]（[[明治]]15年）3月12日 |本殿= |例祭=9月12日 }} [[File:Monument of Sapporo Suwa Shrine.JPG|200px|thumb|建社記念碑]] '''諏訪神社'''（すわじんじゃ）は[[北海道]][[札幌市]][[東区 (札幌市)|東区]]北12条東1丁目1番10号にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は村社。
祭神は[[建御名方神|建御名方命（たけみなかたのみこと）]]と[[八坂刀売神|八坂刀売命（やさかとめのみこと）]]。
平地に建っているにもかかわらず社殿は高い位置にあるが、これは神社前を流れる[[創成川]]の氾濫に備えてのことといわれる<ref name="tsumeku">『爪句 @ 思い出の都市秘境』No.63</ref>。
境内の[[ヤチダモ]]と[[ハルニレ]]の8本の樹木が、札幌市によって保存樹に指定されている<ref>[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html 札幌市東区 - 文化財など]</ref>。
== 歴史 == *[[1878年]]（[[明治]]11年）、[[長野県]]から上島正ら約30名が当地に移住する<ref name="tsumeku" />。 *[[1882年]]（明治15年）3月12日、[[信濃国]]一の宮・[[諏訪大社]]より分霊を勧請して奉る<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html 北海道神社庁 - 諏訪神社]</ref>。 *[[1898年]]（明治31年）2月14日、内務省に出願し無格社となる<ref name="jinjacho" />。 *[[1934年]]（[[昭和]]9年）4月19日、村社に列格。また同年には所在地が[[札幌村]]から札幌市に編入され、これにあわせて社殿と社務所が造営される<ref name="jinjacho" />。 *[[1945年]]（昭和20年）、郷社への昇格を申請するも<ref name="jinjacho" />、実現せず。 *[[1966年]]（昭和41年）、新社殿造営<ref name="jinjacho" />。 *[[1982年]]（昭和57年）、鎮座百年祭<ref name="jinjacho" />。 *[[1992年]]（[[平成]]4年）、鎮座百十年祭。社殿の大屋根葺き替え<ref name="jinjacho" />。 *[[2002年]]（平成14年）、鎮座百二十年祭<ref name="jinjacho" />。 *[[2012年]]（平成24年）、鎮座百三十年祭<ref name="jinjacho" />。

{{神社 |名称=諏訪神社 |画像=[[File:Suwa Shrine of Sapporo.JPG|250px|諏訪神社]]
鳥居と拝殿 {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|43|4|33.5|N|141|21|14.6|E}}}} |所在地=[[北海道]][[札幌市]][[東区 (札幌市)|東区]][[北○条東 (札幌市)|北12条東]]1丁目1番10号 |位置= {{coord|43|4|33.5|N|141|21|14.6|E|display=inline,title}} |社格=旧[[村社]] |祭神=[[建御名方神|建御名方命]]・[[八坂刀売神|八坂刀売命]] |創建=[[1882年]]（[[明治]]15年）3月12日 |本殿= |例祭=9月12日 |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=4|lat_sec=33.5|lon_deg=141|lon_min=21|lon_sec=14.6|label=諏訪神社|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} [[File:Monument of Sapporo Suwa Shrine.JPG|210px|thumb|建社記念碑]] '''諏訪神社'''（すわじんじゃ）は、[[北海道]][[札幌市]][[東区 (札幌市)|東区]][[北○条東 (札幌市)|北12条東]]1丁目1番10号にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[村社]]。'''札幌諏訪神社'''（さっぽろすわじんじゃ）とも。 祭神は[[建御名方神|建御名方命（たけみなかたのみこと）]]と[[八坂刀売神|八坂刀売命（やさかとめのみこと）]]。 平地に建っているにもかかわらず社殿は高い位置にあるが、これは神社前を流れる[[創成川]]の氾濫に備えてのことといわれる『爪句 @ 思い出の都市秘境』No.63。 境内の[[ヤチダモ]]と[[ハルニレ]]の8本の樹木が、札幌市によって保存樹に指定されている[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html 札幌市東区 - 文化財など]。 == 歴史 == *[[1878年]]（[[明治]]11年）、[[長野県]]から[[上島正]]ら約30名が当地に移住する。 *[[1882年]]（明治15年）3月12日、[[信濃国]][[一の宮]]・[[諏訪大社]]より分霊を勧請して奉る[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html 北海道神社庁 - 諏訪神社]。 *[[1898年]]（明治31年）2月14日、[[内務省 (日本)|内務省]]に出願し無格社となる。 *[[1934年]]（[[昭和]]9年）4月19日、[[村社]]に列格。また同年には所在地が[[札幌村]]から[[札幌市]]に編入され、これにあわせて社殿と社務所が造営される。 *[[1945年]]（昭和20年）、郷社への昇格を申請するも、実現せず。 *[[1966年]]（昭和41年）、新社殿造営。 *[[1982年]]（昭和57年）、鎮座百年祭。 *[[1992年]]（[[平成]]4年）、鎮座百十年祭。社殿の大屋根葺き替え。 *[[2002年]]（平成14年）、鎮座百二十年祭。 *[[2012年]]（平成24年）、鎮座百三十年祭。 == 脚注 == == 参考文献 == *青木曲直『爪句 @ 思い出の都市秘境 都市秘境100選ブログ』共同文化社〈北海道豆本 Series8〉、2010年10月7日。ISBN 978-4-87739-187-4 == 外部リンク == *[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html 北海道神社庁 - 諏訪神社] {{デフォルトソート:すわしんしや さつほろ}} [[Category:札幌市の神社]] [[Category:札幌市東区の歴史]] [[Category:札幌市東区の建築物]] [[Category:北海道の旧村社]] [[Category:諏訪神社]]

{{神社 |名称=諏訪神社 |画像=[[File:Suwa Shrine of Sapporo.JPG|250px|諏訪神社]]
鳥居と拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|43|4|33.5|N|141|21|14.6|E}}}} |所在地=[[北海道]][[札幌市]][[東区 (札幌市)|東区]][[北○条東 (札幌市)|北12条東]]1丁目1番10号 |位置= {{coord|43|4|33.5|N|141|21|14.6|E|display=inline,title}} |社格=旧[[村社]] |祭神=[[建御名方神|建御名方命]]・[[八坂刀売神|八坂刀売命]] |創建=[[1882年]]（[[明治]]15年）3月12日 |本殿= |例祭=9月12日 |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=4|lat_sec=33.5|lon_deg=141|lon_min=21|lon_sec=14.6|label=諏訪神社|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} [[File:Monument of Sapporo Suwa Shrine.JPG|210px|thumb|建社記念碑]] '''諏訪神社'''（すわじんじゃ）は、[[北海道]][[札幌市]][[東区 (札幌市)|東区]][[北○条東 (札幌市)|北12条東]]1丁目1番10号にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[村社]]。'''札幌諏訪神社'''（さっぽろすわじんじゃ）とも。 祭神は[[建御名方神|建御名方命（たけみなかたのみこと）]]と[[八坂刀売神|八坂刀売命（やさかとめのみこと）]]。 平地に建っているにもかかわらず社殿は高い位置にあるが、これは神社前を流れる[[創成川]]の氾濫に備えてのことといわれる『爪句 @ 思い出の都市秘境』No.63。 境内の[[ヤチダモ]]と[[ハルニレ]]の8本の樹木が、札幌市によって保存樹に指定されている[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html 札幌市東区 - 文化財など]。 == 歴史 == *[[1878年]]（[[明治]]11年）、[[長野県]]から[[上島正]]ら約30名が当地に移住する。 *[[1882年]]（明治15年）3月12日、[[信濃国]][[一の宮]]・[[諏訪大社]]より分霊を勧請して奉る[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html 北海道神社庁 - 諏訪神社]。 *[[1898年]]（明治31年）2月14日、[[内務省 (日本)|内務省]]に出願し無格社となる。 *[[1934年]]（[[昭和]]9年）4月19日、[[村社]]に列格。また同年には所在地が[[札幌村]]から[[札幌市]]に編入され、これにあわせて社殿と社務所が造営される。 *[[1945年]]（昭和20年）、郷社への昇格を申請するも、実現せず。 *[[1966年]]（昭和41年）、新社殿造営。 *[[1982年]]（昭和57年）、鎮座百年祭。 *[[1992年]]（[[平成]]4年）、鎮座百十年祭。社殿の大屋根葺き替え。 *[[2002年]]（平成14年）、鎮座百二十年祭。 *[[2012年]]（平成24年）、鎮座百三十年祭。 == 脚注 == == 参考文献 == *青木曲直『爪句 @ 思い出の都市秘境 都市秘境100選ブログ』共同文化社〈北海道豆本 Series8〉、2010年10月7日。ISBN 978-4-87739-187-4 == 外部リンク == *[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html 北海道神社庁 - 諏訪神社] {{デフォルトソート:すわしんしや さつほろ}} [[Category:札幌市の神社]] [[Category:札幌市東区の歴史]] [[Category:札幌市東区の建築物]] [[Category:北海道の旧村社]] [[Category:諏訪神社]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Suwa Shrine |Image = [[File:Suwa Shrine of Sapporo.JPG|250px|Suwa Shrine]]
Torii and Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|43|4|33.5|N|141|21|14.6|E}}}} |Location = 1-1-10 Kita 12-jō Higashi, Higashi-ku, Sapporo City, Hokkaido |Coordinates = {{coord|43|4|33.5|N|141|21|14.6|E|display=inline,title}} |Rank = Former {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社}} |Deity = {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売神}} |Founded = March 12, 1882 (Meiji 15) |MainHall = |Festival = September 12 |Map = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=4|lat_sec=33.5|lon_deg=141|lon_min=21|lon_sec=14.6|label=Suwa Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Higashi-ku]], [[Sapporo City]], [[Hokkaido]]. It is also known as '''Sapporo Suwa Shrine''' (札幌諏訪神社). The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売神}}. Although the shrine is located on flat land, its main hall is elevated to protect it from the frequent flooding of the nearby {{ill|Sōsei River|ja|創成川}}.『爪句 @ 思い出の都市秘境』No.63 The shrine grounds are home to eight protected trees, including {{ill|Japanese ash|ja|ヤチダモ}} and {{ill|Japanese elm|ja|ハルニレ}}, designated by Sapporo City.[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties] == History == * **1878 (Meiji 11):** Around 30 settlers from {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, including Ueshima Tadashi, migrated to the area. * **March 12, 1882 (Meiji 15):** A branch of the {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} in Shinano Province was enshrined here.[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine] * **February 14, 1898 (Meiji 31):** Officially recognized as an unranked shrine (無格社). * **April 19, 1934 (Shōwa 9):** Elevated to {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社}} status. That same year, the area was incorporated into Sapporo City, and a new main hall and administration office were constructed. * **1945 (Shōwa 20):** An application for promotion to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} was submitted but not realized. * **1966 (Shōwa 41):** Construction of a new main hall. * **1982 (Shōwa 57):** Centennial celebrations held. * **1992 (Heisei 4):** 110th anniversary and roof repairs to the main hall. * **2002 (Heisei 14):** 120th anniversary celebrated. * **2012 (Heisei 24):** 130th anniversary celebrated. == Features == * **Preserved Trees:** Eight trees, including Japanese ash and Japanese elm, designated for conservation by Sapporo City.[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties] * **Monument:** The shrine grounds include a monument commemorating its establishment. == Annual Events == * **September 12:** The shrine’s annual festival. == External Links == * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine] {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine, Sapporo}} [[Category:Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] [[Category:Sapporo]] [[Category:Former Murasha]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (札幌市)]]

{{Shrine |Name = Suwa Shrine |image = [[File:Suwa Shrine of Sapporo.JPG|250px|Suwa Shrine]]
Torii and Main Hall

That same year, the area was incorporated into Sapporo City, and a new main hall and administration office were constructed. * **1945 (Shōwa 20):** An application for promotion to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} was submitted but not realized. * **1966 (Shōwa 41):** Construction of a new main hall. * **1982 (Shōwa 57):** Centennial celebrations held. * **1992 (Heisei 4):** 110th anniversary and roof repairs to the main hall. * **2002 (Heisei 14):** 120th anniversary celebrated. * **2012 (Heisei 24):** 130th anniversary celebrated. == Features == * **Preserved Trees:** Eight trees, including Japanese ash and Japanese elm, designated for conservation by Sapporo City.[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties] * **Monument:** The shrine grounds include a monument commemorating its establishment. == Annual Events == * **September 12:** The shrine’s annual festival. == External Links == * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine] {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine, Sapporo}} [[Category:Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] [[Category:Sapporo]] [[Category:Former Murasha]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (札幌市)]]

{{Shrine |Name = Suwa Shrine |image = [[File:Suwa Shrine of Sapporo.JPG|250px|Suwa Shrine]]
Torii and Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|43|4|33.5|N|141|21|14.6|E}}}} |location = 1-1-10 Kita 12-jō Higashi, Higashi-ku, Sapporo City, Hokkaido |Coordinates = {{coord|43|4|33.5|N|141|21|14.6|E|display=inline,title}} |Rank = Former {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社}} |deity = {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売神}} |Founded = March 12, 1882 (Meiji 15) |MainHall = |Festival = September 12 |Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=4|lat_sec=33.5|lon_deg=141|lon_min=21|lon_sec=14.6|label=Suwa Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Higashi-ku]], [[Sapporo City]], [[Hokkaido]]. It is also known as '''Sapporo Suwa Shrine''' (札幌諏訪神社). The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売神}}. Although the shrine is located on flat land, its main hall is elevated to protect it from the frequent flooding of the nearby {{ill|Sōsei River|ja|創成川}}.『爪句 @ 思い出の都市秘境』No.63 The shrine grounds are home to eight protected trees, including {{ill|Japanese ash|ja|ヤチダモ}} and {{ill|Japanese elm|ja|ハルニレ}}, designated by Sapporo City.[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties] == History == * **1878 (Meiji 11):** Around 30 settlers from {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, including Ueshima Tadashi, migrated to the area. * **March 12, 1882 (Meiji 15):** A branch of the {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} in Shinano Province was enshrined here.[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine] * **February 14, 1898 (Meiji 31):** Officially recognized as an unranked shrine (無格社). * **April 19, 1934 (Shōwa 9):** Elevated to {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社}} status. That same year, the area was incorporated into Sapporo City, and a new main hall and administration office were constructed. * **1945 (Shōwa 20):** An application for promotion to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} was submitted but not realized. * **1966 (Shōwa 41):** Construction of a new main hall. * **1982 (Shōwa 57):** Centennial celebrations held. * **1992 (Heisei 4):** 110th anniversary and roof repairs to the main hall. * **2002 (Heisei 14):** 120th anniversary celebrated. * **2012 (Heisei 24):** 130th anniversary celebrated. == Features == * **Preserved Trees:** Eight trees, including Japanese ash and Japanese elm, designated for conservation by Sapporo City.[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties] * **Monument:** The shrine grounds include a monument commemorating its establishment. == Annual Events == * **September 12:** The shrine’s annual festival. == External Links == * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine] {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine, Sapporo}} [[Category:Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] [[Category:Sapporo]] [[Category:Former Murasha]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[category:most recent 500]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (札幌市)]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|43|4|33.5|N|141|21|14.6|E}}}} |location = 1-1-10 Kita 12-jō Higashi, Higashi-ku, Sapporo City, Hokkaido |coordinates = {{coord|43|4|33.5|N|141|21|14.6|E|display=inline,title}} |Rank = Former {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社}} |deity = {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売神}} |consecrated = March 12, 1882 (Meiji 15) |MainHall = |Festival = September 12 |Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=4|lat_sec=33.5|lon_deg=141|lon_min=21|lon_sec=14.6|label=Suwa Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Higashi-ku]], [[Sapporo City]], [[Hokkaido]]. It is also known as '''Sapporo Suwa Shrine''' (札幌諏訪神社). The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売神}}. Although the shrine is located on flat land, its main hall is elevated to protect it from the frequent flooding of the nearby {{ill|Sōsei River|ja|創成川}}.『爪句 @ 思い出の都市秘境』No.63 The shrine grounds are home to eight protected trees, including {{ill|Japanese ash|ja|ヤチダモ}} and {{ill|Japanese elm|ja|ハルニレ}}, designated by Sapporo City.[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties] == History == * '''1878 (Meiji 11):''' Around 30 settlers from {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, including Ueshima Tadashi, migrated to the area. * '''March 12, 1882 (Meiji 15):''' A branch of the {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} in Shinano Province was enshrined here.[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine] * '''February 14, 1898 (Meiji 31):''' Officially recognized as an unranked shrine (無格社). * '''April 19, 1934 (Shōwa 9):''' Elevated to {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社}} status. That same year, the area was incorporated into Sapporo City, and a new main hall and administration office were constructed. * '''1945 (Shōwa 20):''' An application for promotion to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} was submitted but not realized. * '''1966 (Shōwa 41):''' Construction of a new main hall. * '''1982 (Shōwa 57):''' Centennial celebrations held. * '''1992 (Heisei 4):''' 110th anniversary and roof repairs to the main hall. * '''2002 (Heisei 14):''' 120th anniversary celebrated. * '''2012 (Heisei 24):''' 130th anniversary celebrated. == Features == * '''Preserved Trees:''' Eight trees, including Japanese ash and Japanese elm, designated for conservation by Sapporo City.[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties] * '''Monument:''' The shrine grounds include a monument commemorating its establishment. == Annual Events == * '''September 12:''' The shrine’s annual festival. == External Links == * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine] {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine, Sapporo}} [[Category:Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] [[Category:Sapporo]] [[Category:Former Murasha]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:New pages]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (札幌市)]]

Although the shrine is located on flat land, its main hall is elevated to protect it from the frequent flooding of the nearby {{ill|Sōsei River|ja|創成川}}.『爪句 @ 思い出の都市秘境』No.63 The shrine grounds are home to eight protected trees, including {{ill|Japanese ash|ja|ヤチダモ}} and {{ill|Japanese elm|ja|ハルニレ}}, designated by Sapporo City.[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties] == History == * '''1878 (Meiji 11):''' Around 30 settlers from {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, including Ueshima Tadashi, migrated to the area. * '''March 12, 1882 (Meiji 15):''' A branch of the {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} in Shinano Province was enshrined here.[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine] * '''February 14, 1898 (Meiji 31):''' Officially recognized as an unranked shrine (無格社). * '''April 19, 1934 (Shōwa 9):''' Elevated to {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社}} status. That same year, the area was incorporated into Sapporo City, and a new main hall and administration office were constructed. * '''1945 (Shōwa 20):''' An application for promotion to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} was submitted but not realized. * '''1966 (Shōwa 41):''' Construction of a new main hall. * '''1982 (Shōwa 57):''' Centennial celebrations held. * '''1992 (Heisei 4):''' 110th anniversary and roof repairs to the main hall. * '''2002 (Heisei 14):''' 120th anniversary celebrated. * '''2012 (Heisei 24):''' 130th anniversary celebrated. == Features == * '''Preserved Trees:''' Eight trees, including Japanese ash and Japanese elm, designated for conservation by Sapporo City.[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties] * '''Monument:''' The shrine grounds include a monument commemorating its establishment. == Annual Events == * '''September 12:''' The shrine’s annual festival. == External Links == * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine] {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine, Sapporo}} [[Category:Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] [[Category:Sapporo]] [[Category:Former Murasha]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (札幌市)]]

It is also known as '''Sapporo Suwa Shrine''' (札幌諏訪神社). The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売神}}. Although the shrine is located on flat land, its main hall is elevated to protect it from the frequent flooding of the nearby {{ill|Sōsei River|ja|創成川}}.『爪句 @ 思い出の都市秘境』No.63 The shrine grounds are home to eight protected trees, including {{ill|Japanese ash|ja|ヤチダモ}} and {{ill|Japanese elm|ja|ハルニレ}}, designated by Sapporo City.[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties] == History == * '''1878 (Meiji 11):''' Around 30 settlers from {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, including Ueshima Tadashi, migrated to the area. * '''March 12, 1882 (Meiji 15):''' A branch of the {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} in Shinano Province was enshrined here.[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine] * '''February 14, 1898 (Meiji 31):''' Officially recognized as an unranked shrine (無格社). * '''April 19, 1934 (Shōwa 9):''' Elevated to {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社}} status. That same year, the area was incorporated into Sapporo City, and a new main hall and administration office were constructed. * '''1945 (Shōwa 20):''' An application for promotion to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} was submitted but not realized. * '''1966 (Shōwa 41):''' Construction of a new main hall. * '''1982 (Shōwa 57):''' Centennial celebrations held. * '''1992 (Heisei 4):''' 110th anniversary and roof repairs to the main hall. * '''2002 (Heisei 14):''' 120th anniversary celebrated. * '''2012 (Heisei 24):''' 130th anniversary celebrated. == Features == * '''Preserved Trees:''' Eight trees, including Japanese ash and Japanese elm, designated for conservation by Sapporo City.[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties] * '''Monument:''' The shrine grounds include a monument commemorating its establishment. == Annual Events == * '''September 12:''' The shrine’s annual festival. == External Links == * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine] {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine, Sapporo}} [[Category:Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] [[Category:Sapporo]] [[Category:Former Murasha]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)-qq]] [[Category:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]] [[Category:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[ja:諏訪神社 (札幌市)]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Suwa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Suwa Shrine of Sapporo.JPG|250px|Suwa Shrine]]
Torii and Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|43|4|33.5|N|141|21|14.6|E}}}} |location = 1-1-10 Kita 12-jō Higashi, Higashi-ku, Sapporo City, Hokkaido |coordinates = {{coord|43|4|33.5|N|141|21|14.6|E|display=inline,title}} |Rank = Former {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社}} |deity = {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売神}} |consecration_year = March 12, 1882 (Meiji 15) |MainHall = |Festival = September 12 |Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=4|lat_sec=33.5|lon_deg=141|lon_min=21|lon_sec=14.6|label=Suwa Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Higashi-ku]], [[Sapporo City]], [[Hokkaido]]. It is also known as '''Sapporo Suwa Shrine''' (札幌諏訪神社). The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売神}}. Although the shrine is located on flat land, its main hall is elevated to protect it from the frequent flooding of the nearby {{ill|Sōsei River|ja|創成川}}.『爪句 @ 思い出の都市秘境』No.63 The shrine grounds are home to eight protected trees, including {{ill|Japanese ash|ja|ヤチダモ}} and {{ill|Japanese elm|ja|ハルニレ}}, designated by Sapporo City.[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties] == History == * '''1878 (Meiji 11):''' Around 30 settlers from {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, including Ueshima Tadashi, migrated to the area. * '''March 12, 1882 (Meiji 15):''' A branch of the {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} in Shinano Province was enshrined here.[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine] * '''February 14, 1898 (Meiji 31):''' Officially recognized as an unranked shrine (無格社). * '''April 19, 1934 (Shōwa 9):''' Elevated to {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社}} status. That same year, the area was incorporated into Sapporo City, and a new main hall and administration office were constructed. * '''1945 (Shōwa 20):''' An application for promotion to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} was submitted but not realized. * '''1966 (Shōwa 41):''' Construction of a new main hall. * '''1982 (Shōwa 57):''' Centennial celebrations held. * '''1992 (Heisei 4):''' 110th anniversary and roof repairs to the main hall. * '''2002 (Heisei 14):''' 120th anniversary celebrated. * '''2012 (Heisei 24):''' 130th anniversary celebrated. == Features == * '''Preserved Trees:''' Eight trees, including Japanese ash and Japanese elm, designated for conservation by Sapporo City.[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties] * '''Monument:''' The shrine grounds include a monument commemorating its establishment. == Annual Events == * '''September 12:''' The shrine’s annual festival. == External Links == * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine] {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine, Sapporo}} [[Category:Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] [[Category:Sapporo]] [[Category:Former Murasha]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)-qq]] [[Category:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]] [[Category:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (札幌市)]]

That same year, the area was incorporated into Sapporo City, and a new main hall and administration office were constructed. * '''1945 (Shōwa 20):''' An application for promotion to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} was submitted but not realized. * '''1966 (Shōwa 41):''' Construction of a new main hall. * '''1982 (Shōwa 57):''' Centennial celebrations held. * '''1992 (Heisei 4):''' 110th anniversary and roof repairs to the main hall. * '''2002 (Heisei 14):''' 120th anniversary celebrated. * '''2012 (Heisei 24):''' 130th anniversary celebrated. == Features == * '''Preserved Trees:''' Eight trees, including Japanese ash and Japanese elm, designated for conservation by Sapporo City.[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties] * '''Monument:''' The shrine grounds include a monument commemorating its establishment. == Annual Events == * '''September 12:''' The shrine’s annual festival. == External Links == * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Although the shrine is located on flat land, its main hall is elevated to protect it from the frequent flooding of the nearby {{ill|Sōsei River|ja|創成川}}.『爪句 @ 思い出の都市秘境』No.63 The shrine grounds are home to eight protected trees, including {{ill|Japanese ash|ja|ヤチダモ}} and {{ill|Japanese elm|ja|ハルニレ}}, designated by Sapporo City.[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties] == History == * '''1878 (Meiji 11):''' Around 30 settlers from {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, including Ueshima Tadashi, migrated to the area. * '''March 12, 1882 (Meiji 15):''' A branch of the {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} in Shinano Province was enshrined here.[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine] * '''February 14, 1898 (Meiji 31):''' Officially recognized as an unranked shrine (無格社). * '''April 19, 1934 (Shōwa 9):''' Elevated to {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社}} status. That same year, the area was incorporated into Sapporo City, and a new main hall and administration office were constructed. * '''1945 (Shōwa 20):''' An application for promotion to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} was submitted but not realized. * '''1966 (Shōwa 41):''' Construction of a new main hall. * '''1982 (Shōwa 57):''' Centennial celebrations held. * '''1992 (Heisei 4):''' 110th anniversary and roof repairs to the main hall. * '''2002 (Heisei 14):''' 120th anniversary celebrated. * '''2012 (Heisei 24):''' 130th anniversary celebrated. == Features == * '''Preserved Trees:''' Eight trees, including Japanese ash and Japanese elm, designated for conservation by Sapporo City.[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties] * '''Monument:''' The shrine grounds include a monument commemorating its establishment. == Annual Events == * '''September 12:''' The shrine’s annual festival. == External Links == * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine] {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine, Sapporo}} [[Category:Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] [[Category:Sapporo]] [[Category:Former Murasha]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]] [[Category:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:uwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (札幌市)]]

It is also known as '''Sapporo Suwa Shrine''' (札幌諏訪神社). The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売神}}. Although the shrine is located on flat land, its main hall is elevated to protect it from the frequent flooding of the nearby {{ill|Sōsei River|ja|創成川}}.『爪句 @ 思い出の都市秘境』No.63 The shrine grounds are home to eight protected trees, including {{ill|Japanese ash|ja|ヤチダモ}} and {{ill|Japanese elm|ja|ハルニレ}}, designated by Sapporo City.[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties] == History == * '''1878 (Meiji 11):''' Around 30 settlers from {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, including Ueshima Tadashi, migrated to the area. * '''March 12, 1882 (Meiji 15):''' A branch of the {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} in Shinano Province was enshrined here.[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine] * '''February 14, 1898 (Meiji 31):''' Officially recognized as an unranked shrine (無格社). * '''April 19, 1934 (Shōwa 9):''' Elevated to {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社}} status. That same year, the area was incorporated into Sapporo City, and a new main hall and administration office were constructed. * '''1945 (Shōwa 20):''' An application for promotion to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} was submitted but not realized. * '''1966 (Shōwa 41):''' Construction of a new main hall. * '''1982 (Shōwa 57):''' Centennial celebrations held. * '''1992 (Heisei 4):''' 110th anniversary and roof repairs to the main hall. * '''2002 (Heisei 14):''' 120th anniversary celebrated. * '''2012 (Heisei 24):''' 130th anniversary celebrated. == Features == * '''Preserved Trees:''' Eight trees, including Japanese ash and Japanese elm, designated for conservation by Sapporo City.[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties] * '''Monument:''' The shrine grounds include a monument commemorating its establishment. == Annual Events == * '''September 12:''' The shrine’s annual festival. == External Links == * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Torii and Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|43|4|33.5|N|141|21|14.6|E}}}} |location = 1-1-10 Kita 12-jō Higashi, Higashi-ku, Sapporo City, Hokkaido |coordinates = {{coord|43|4|33.5|N|141|21|14.6|E|display=inline,title}} |Rank = Former {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社}} |deity = {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売神}} |consecration_year = March 12, 1882 (Meiji 15) |MainHall = |Festival = September 12 |Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=4|lat_sec=33.5|lon_deg=141|lon_min=21|lon_sec=14.6|label=Suwa Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Higashi-ku]], [[Sapporo City]], [[Hokkaido]]. It is also known as '''Sapporo Suwa Shrine''' (札幌諏訪神社). The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売神}}. Although the shrine is located on flat land, its main hall is elevated to protect it from the frequent flooding of the nearby {{ill|Sōsei River|ja|創成川}}.『爪句 @ 思い出の都市秘境』No.63 The shrine grounds are home to eight protected trees, including {{ill|Japanese ash|ja|ヤチダモ}} and {{ill|Japanese elm|ja|ハルニレ}}, designated by Sapporo City.[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties] == History == * '''1878 (Meiji 11):''' Around 30 settlers from {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, including Ueshima Tadashi, migrated to the area. * '''March 12, 1882 (Meiji 15):''' A branch of the {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} in Shinano Province was enshrined here.[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine] * '''February 14, 1898 (Meiji 31):''' Officially recognized as an unranked shrine (無格社). * '''April 19, 1934 (Shōwa 9):''' Elevated to {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社}} status. That same year, the area was incorporated into Sapporo City, and a new main hall and administration office were constructed. * '''1945 (Shōwa 20):''' An application for promotion to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} was submitted but not realized. * '''1966 (Shōwa 41):''' Construction of a new main hall. * '''1982 (Shōwa 57):''' Centennial celebrations held. * '''1992 (Heisei 4):''' 110th anniversary and roof repairs to the main hall. * '''2002 (Heisei 14):''' 120th anniversary celebrated. * '''2012 (Heisei 24):''' 130th anniversary celebrated. == Features == * '''Preserved Trees:''' Eight trees, including Japanese ash and Japanese elm, designated for conservation by Sapporo City.[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties] * '''Monument:''' The shrine grounds include a monument commemorating its establishment. == Annual Events == * '''September 12:''' The shrine’s annual festival. == External Links == * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine] {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine, Sapporo}} [[Category:Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] [[Category:Sapporo]] [[Category:Former Murasha]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]] [[Category:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:uwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (札幌市)]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Suwa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Suwa Shrine of Sapporo.JPG|250px|Suwa Shrine]]
Torii and Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|43|4|33.5|N|141|21|14.6|E}}}} |location = 1-1-10 Kita 12-jō Higashi, Higashi-ku, Sapporo City, Hokkaido |coordinates = {{coord|43|4|33.5|N|141|21|14.6|E|display=inline,title}} |Rank = Former {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社}} |deity = {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売神}} |consecration_year = March 12, 1882 (Meiji 15) |MainHall = |Festival = September 12 |Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=4|lat_sec=33.5|lon_deg=141|lon_min=21|lon_sec=14.6|label=Suwa Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Higashi-ku]], [[Sapporo City]], [[Hokkaido]]. It is also known as '''Sapporo Suwa Shrine''' (札幌諏訪神社). The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売神}}. Although the shrine is located on flat land, its main hall is elevated to protect it from the frequent flooding of the nearby {{ill|Sōsei River|ja|創成川}}.『爪句 @ 思い出の都市秘境』No.63 The shrine grounds are home to eight protected trees, including {{ill|Japanese ash|ja|ヤチダモ}} and {{ill|Japanese elm|ja|ハルニレ}}, designated by Sapporo City.[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties] == History == * '''1878 (Meiji 11):''' Around 30 settlers from {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, including Ueshima Tadashi, migrated to the area. * '''March 12, 1882 (Meiji 15):''' A branch of the {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} in Shinano Province was enshrined here.[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine] * '''February 14, 1898 (Meiji 31):''' Officially recognized as an unranked shrine (無格社). * '''April 19, 1934 (Shōwa 9):''' Elevated to {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社}} status. That same year, the area was incorporated into Sapporo City, and a new main hall and administration office were constructed. * '''1945 (Shōwa 20):''' An application for promotion to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} was submitted but not realized. * '''1966 (Shōwa 41):''' Construction of a new main hall. * '''1982 (Shōwa 57):''' Centennial celebrations held. * '''1992 (Heisei 4):''' 110th anniversary and roof repairs to the main hall. * '''2002 (Heisei 14):''' 120th anniversary celebrated. * '''2012 (Heisei 24):''' 130th anniversary celebrated. == Features == * '''Preserved Trees:''' Eight trees, including Japanese ash and Japanese elm, designated for conservation by Sapporo City.[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties] * '''Monument:''' The shrine grounds include a monument commemorating its establishment. == Annual Events == * '''September 12:''' The shrine’s annual festival. == External Links == * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine] {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine, Sapporo}} [[Category:Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] [[Category:Sapporo]] [[Category:Former Murasha]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]] [[Category:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:uwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (札幌市)]] [[Category:New]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Suwa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Suwa Shrine of Sapporo.JPG|250px|Suwa Shrine]]
Torii and Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|43|4|33.5|N|141|21|14.6|E}}}} |location = 1-1-10 Kita 12-jō Higashi, Higashi-ku, Sapporo City, Hokkaido |coordinates = {{coord|43|4|33.5|N|141|21|14.6|E|display=inline,title}} |Rank = Former {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社}} |deity = {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売神}} |consecration_year = March 12, 1882 (Meiji 15) |MainHall = |Festival = September 12 |Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=4|lat_sec=33.5|lon_deg=141|lon_min=21|lon_sec=14.6|label=Suwa Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Higashi-ku]], [[Sapporo City]], [[Hokkaido]]. It is also known as '''Sapporo Suwa Shrine''' (札幌諏訪神社). The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売神}}. Although the shrine is located on flat land, its main hall is elevated to protect it from the frequent flooding of the nearby {{ill|Sōsei River|ja|創成川}}.『爪句 @ 思い出の都市秘境』No.63 The shrine grounds are home to eight protected trees, including {{ill|Japanese ash|ja|ヤチダモ}} and {{ill|Japanese elm|ja|ハルニレ}}, designated by Sapporo City.[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties] == History == * '''1878 (Meiji 11):''' Around 30 settlers from {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, including Ueshima Tadashi, migrated to the area. * '''March 12, 1882 (Meiji 15):''' A branch of the {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} in Shinano Province was enshrined here.[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine] * '''February 14, 1898 (Meiji 31):''' Officially recognized as an unranked shrine (無格社). * '''April 19, 1934 (Shōwa 9):''' Elevated to {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社}} status. That same year, the area was incorporated into Sapporo City, and a new main hall and administration office were constructed. * '''1945 (Shōwa 20):''' An application for promotion to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} was submitted but not realized. * '''1966 (Shōwa 41):''' Construction of a new main hall. * '''1982 (Shōwa 57):''' Centennial celebrations held. * '''1992 (Heisei 4):''' 110th anniversary and roof repairs to the main hall. * '''2002 (Heisei 14):''' 120th anniversary celebrated. * '''2012 (Heisei 24):''' 130th anniversary celebrated. == Features == * '''Preserved Trees:''' Eight trees, including Japanese ash and Japanese elm, designated for conservation by Sapporo City.[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties] * '''Monument:''' The shrine grounds include a monument commemorating its establishment. == Annual Events == * '''September 12:''' The shrine’s annual festival. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine] {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine, Sapporo}} [[Category:Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] [[Category:Sapporo]] [[Category:Former Murasha]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]] [[Category:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:uwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (札幌市)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|43|4|33.5|N|141|21|14.6|E}}}} |location = 1-1-10 Kita 12-jō Higashi, Higashi-ku, Sapporo City, Hokkaido |coordinates = {{coord|43|4|33.5|N|141|21|14.6|E|display=inline,title}} |Rank = Former {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社|12=draft}} |deity = {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方神|12=draft}} and {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売神|12=draft}} |consecration_year = March 12, 1882 (Meiji 15) |MainHall = |Festival = September 12 |Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=4|lat_sec=33.5|lon_deg=141|lon_min=21|lon_sec=14.6|label=Suwa Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Higashi-ku]], [[Sapporo City]], [[Hokkaido]]. Although the shrine is located on flat land, its main hall is elevated to protect it from the frequent flooding of the nearby {{ill|Sōsei River|ja|創成川|12=draft}}.『爪句 @ 思い出の都市秘境』No.63 The shrine grounds are home to eight protected trees, including {{ill|Japanese ash|ja|ヤチダモ|12=draft}} and {{ill|Japanese elm|ja|ハルニレ|12=draft}}, designated by Sapporo City.[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties] == History == * '''1878 (Meiji 11):''' Around 30 settlers from {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}}, including Ueshima Tadashi, migrated to the area. * '''March 12, 1882 (Meiji 15):''' A branch of the {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft}} in Shinano Province was enshrined here.[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine] * '''February 14, 1898 (Meiji 31):''' Officially recognized as an unranked shrine (無格社). * '''April 19, 1934 (Shōwa 9):''' Elevated to {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社|12=draft}} status. That same year, the area was incorporated into Sapporo City, and a new main hall and administration office were constructed. * '''1945 (Shōwa 20):''' An application for promotion to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} was submitted but not realized. * '''1966 (Shōwa 41):''' Construction of a new main hall. * '''1982 (Shōwa 57):''' Centennial celebrations held. * '''1992 (Heisei 4):''' 110th anniversary and roof repairs to the main hall. * '''2002 (Heisei 14):''' 120th anniversary celebrated. * '''2012 (Heisei 24):''' 130th anniversary celebrated. == Features == * '''Preserved Trees:''' Eight trees, including Japanese ash and Japanese elm, designated for conservation by Sapporo City.[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties] * '''Monument:''' The shrine grounds include a monument commemorating its establishment. == Annual Events == * '''September 12:''' The shrine’s annual festival. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] [[Category:Sapporo]] [[Category:Former Murasha]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]] [[sn:uwa Shrine (Sapporo)]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (札幌市)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine, Sapporo}}

Torii and Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|43|4|33.5|N|141|21|14.6|E}}}} |location = 1-1-10 Kita 12-jō Higashi, Higashi-ku, Sapporo City, Hokkaido |coordinates = {{coord|43|4|33.5|N|141|21|14.6|E|display=inline,title}} |Rank = Former {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |deity = {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |consecration_year = March 12, 1882 (Meiji 15) |MainHall = |Festival = September 12 |Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=4|lat_sec=33.5|lon_deg=141|lon_min=21|lon_sec=14.6|label=Suwa Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Higashi-ku]], [[Sapporo City]], [[Hokkaido]]. It is also known as '''Sapporo Suwa Shrine''' (札幌諏訪神社). The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Although the shrine is located on flat land, its main hall is elevated to protect it from the frequent flooding of the nearby {{ill|Sōsei River|ja|創成川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.『爪句 @ 思い出の都市秘境』No.63 The shrine grounds are home to eight protected trees, including {{ill|Japanese ash|ja|ヤチダモ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Japanese elm|ja|ハルニレ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, designated by Sapporo City.[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties] == History == * '''1878 (Meiji 11):''' Around 30 settlers from {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, including Ueshima Tadashi, migrated to the area. * '''March 12, 1882 (Meiji 15):''' A branch of the {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Shinano Province was enshrined here.[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine] * '''February 14, 1898 (Meiji 31):''' Officially recognized as an unRanked shrine (無格社). * '''April 19, 1934 (Shōwa 9):''' Elevated to {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} status. That same year, the area was incorporated into Sapporo City, and a new main hall and administration office were constructed. * '''1945 (Shōwa 20):''' An application for promotion to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was submitted but not realized. * '''1966 (Shōwa 41):''' Construction of a new main hall. * '''1982 (Shōwa 57):''' Centennial celebrations held. * '''1992 (Heisei 4):''' 110th anniversary and roof repairs to the main hall. * '''2002 (Heisei 14):''' 120th anniversary celebrated. * '''2012 (Heisei 24):''' 130th anniversary celebrated. == Features == * '''Preserved Trees:''' Eight trees, including Japanese ash and Japanese elm, designated for conservation by Sapporo City.[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties] * '''Monument:''' The shrine grounds include a monument commemorating its establishment. == Annual Events == * '''September 12:''' The shrine’s annual festival. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] [[Category:Sapporo]] [[Category:Former Murasha]] [[Category:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]] [[sn:uwa Shrine (Sapporo)]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (札幌市)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine, Sapporo}}

That same year, the area was incorporated into Sapporo City, and a new main hall and administration office were constructed. * '''1945 (Shōwa 20):''' An application for promotion to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was submitted but not realized. * '''1966 (Shōwa 41):''' Construction of a new main hall. * '''1982 (Shōwa 57):''' Centennial celebrations held. * '''1992 (Heisei 4):''' 110th anniversary and roof repairs to the main hall. * '''2002 (Heisei 14):''' 120th anniversary celebrated. * '''2012 (Heisei 24):''' 130th anniversary celebrated. == Features == * '''Preserved Trees:''' Eight trees, including Japanese ash and Japanese elm, designated for conservation by Sapporo City.[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties] * '''Monument:''' The shrine grounds include a monument commemorating its establishment. == Annual Events == * '''September 12:''' The shrine’s annual festival. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] [[Category:Sapporo]] [[Category:Former Murasha]] [[Category:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]] [[sn:uwa Shrine (Sapporo)]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (札幌市)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine, Sapporo}}

revision.comment: 13 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:諏訪神社 (札幌市) up to rev 101918338
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=諏訪神社 |画像=[[File:Suwa Shrine of Sapporo.JPG|250px|諏訪神社]]
鳥居と拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|43|4|33.5|N|141|21|14.6|E}}}} |所在地=[[北海道]][[札幌市]][[東区 (札幌市)|東区]][[北○条東 (札幌市)|北12条東]]1丁目1番10号 |位置= {{coord|43|4|33.5|N|141|21|14.6|E|display=inline,title}} |社格=旧[[村社]] |祭神=[[建御名方神|建御名方命]]・[[八坂刀売神|八坂刀売命]] |創建=[[1882年]]（[[明治]]15年）3月12日 |本殿= |例祭=9月12日 |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=4|lat_sec=33.5|lon_deg=141|lon_min=21|lon_sec=14.6|label=諏訪神社|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} [[File:Monument of Sapporo Suwa Shrine.JPG|210px|thumb|建社記念碑]] '''諏訪神社'''（すわじんじゃ）は、[[北海道]][[札幌市]][[東区 (札幌市)|東区]][[北○条東 (札幌市)|北12条東]]1丁目1番10号にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[村社]]。'''札幌諏訪神社'''（さっぽろすわじんじゃ）とも。 祭神は[[建御名方神|建御名方命（たけみなかたのみこと）]]と[[八坂刀売神|八坂刀売命（やさかとめのみこと）]]。 平地に建っているにもかかわらず社殿は高い位置にあるが、これは神社前を流れる[[創成川]]の氾濫に備えてのことといわれる『爪句 @ 思い出の都市秘境』No.63。 境内の[[ヤチダモ]]と[[ハルニレ]]の8本の樹木が、札幌市によって保存樹に指定されている[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html 札幌市東区 - 文化財など]。 == 歴史 == *[[1878年]]（[[明治]]11年）、[[長野県]]から[[上島正]]ら約30名が当地に移住する。 *[[1882年]]（明治15年）3月12日、[[信濃国]][[一の宮]]・[[諏訪大社]]より分霊を勧請して奉る[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html 北海道神社庁 - 諏訪神社]。 *[[1898年]]（明治31年）2月14日、[[内務省 (日本)|内務省]]に出願し無格社となる。 *[[1934年]]（[[昭和]]9年）4月19日、[[村社]]に列格。また同年には所在地が[[札幌村]]から[[札幌市]]に編入され、これにあわせて社殿と社務所が造営される。 *[[1945年]]（昭和20年）、郷社への昇格を申請するも、実現せず。 *[[1966年]]（昭和41年）、新社殿造営。 *[[1982年]]（昭和57年）、鎮座百年祭。 *[[1992年]]（[[平成]]4年）、鎮座百十年祭。社殿の大屋根葺き替え。 *[[2002年]]（平成14年）、鎮座百二十年祭。 *[[2012年]]（平成24年）、鎮座百三十年祭。 == 脚注 == == 参考文献 == *青木曲直『爪句 @ 思い出の都市秘境 都市秘境100選ブログ』共同文化社〈北海道豆本 Series8〉、2010年10月7日。ISBN 978-4-87739-187-4 == 外部リンク == *[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html 北海道神社庁 - 諏訪神社] {{デフォルトソート:すわしんしや さつほろ}} [[Category:札幌市の神社]] [[Category:札幌市東区の歴史]] [[Category:札幌市東区の建築物]] [[Category:北海道の旧村社]] [[Category:諏訪神社]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Suwa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Suwa Shrine of Sapporo.JPG|250px|Suwa Shrine]]
Torii and Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|43|4|33.5|N|141|21|14.6|E}}}} |location = 1-1-10 Kita 12-jō Higashi, Higashi-ku, Sapporo City, Hokkaido |coordinates = {{coord|43|4|33.5|N|141|21|14.6|E|display=inline,title}} |Rank = Former {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |deity = {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |consecration_year = March 12, 1882 (Meiji 15) |MainHall = |Festival = September 12 |Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=4|lat_sec=33.5|lon_deg=141|lon_min=21|lon_sec=14.6|label=Suwa Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Higashi-ku]], [[Sapporo City]], [[Hokkaido]]. It is also known as '''Sapporo Suwa Shrine''' (札幌諏訪神社). The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Although the shrine is located on flat land, its main hall is elevated to protect it from the frequent flooding of the nearby {{ill|Sōsei River|ja|創成川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.『爪句 @ 思い出の都市秘境』No.63 The shrine grounds are home to eight protected trees, including {{ill|Japanese ash|ja|ヤチダモ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Japanese elm|ja|ハルニレ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, designated by Sapporo City.[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties] == History == * '''1878 (Meiji 11):''' Around 30 settlers from {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, including Ueshima Tadashi, migrated to the area. * '''March 12, 1882 (Meiji 15):''' A branch of the {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Shinano Province was enshrined here.[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine] * '''February 14, 1898 (Meiji 31):''' Officially recognized as an unranked shrine (無格社). * '''April 19, 1934 (Shōwa 9):''' Elevated to {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} status. That same year, the area was incorporated into Sapporo City, and a new main hall and administration office were constructed. * '''1945 (Shōwa 20):''' An application for promotion to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was submitted but not realized. * '''1966 (Shōwa 41):''' Construction of a new main hall. * '''1982 (Shōwa 57):''' Centennial celebrations held. * '''1992 (Heisei 4):''' 110th anniversary and roof repairs to the main hall. * '''2002 (Heisei 14):''' 120th anniversary celebrated. * '''2012 (Heisei 24):''' 130th anniversary celebrated. == Features == * '''Preserved Trees:''' Eight trees, including Japanese ash and Japanese elm, designated for conservation by Sapporo City.[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties] * '''Monument:''' The shrine grounds include a monument commemorating its establishment. == Annual Events == * '''September 12:''' The shrine’s annual festival. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] [[Category:Sapporo]] [[Category:Former Murasha]] [[Category:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]] [[sn:uwa Shrine (Sapporo)]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (札幌市)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Suwa Shrine, Sapporo}} {{translated page|ja|諏訪神社 (札幌市)|version=101918338|comment=Imported full ja history}}

That same year, the area was incorporated into Sapporo City, and a new main hall and administration office were constructed. * '''1945 (Shōwa 20):''' An application for promotion to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was submitted but not realized. * '''1966 (Shōwa 41):''' Construction of a new main hall. * '''1982 (Shōwa 57):''' Centennial celebrations held. * '''1992 (Heisei 4):''' 110th anniversary and roof repairs to the main hall. * '''2002 (Heisei 14):''' 120th anniversary celebrated. * '''2012 (Heisei 24):''' 130th anniversary celebrated. == Features == * '''Preserved Trees:''' Eight trees, including Japanese ash and Japanese elm, designated for conservation by Sapporo City.[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties] * '''Monument:''' The shrine grounds include a monument commemorating its establishment. == Annual Events == * '''September 12:''' The shrine’s annual festival. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine] {{translated page|ja|諏訪神社 (札幌市)|version=101918338|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] [[Category:Sapporo]] [[Category:Former Unranked Shrines]] [[Category:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]] [[sn:uwa Shrine (Sapporo)]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (札幌市)]]

That same year, the area was incorporated into Sapporo City, and a new main hall and administration office were constructed. * '''1945 (Shōwa 20):''' An application for promotion to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} was submitted but not realized. * '''1966 (Shōwa 41):''' Construction of a new main hall. * '''1982 (Shōwa 57):''' Centennial celebrations held. * '''1992 (Heisei 4):''' 110th anniversary and roof repairs to the main hall. * '''2002 (Heisei 14):''' 120th anniversary celebrated. * '''2012 (Heisei 24):''' 130th anniversary celebrated. == Features == * '''Preserved Trees:''' Eight trees, including Japanese ash and Japanese elm, designated for conservation by Sapporo City.[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties] * '''Monument:''' The shrine grounds include a monument commemorating its establishment. == Annual Events == * '''September 12:''' The shrine’s annual festival. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine] {{translated page|ja|諏訪神社 (札幌市)|version=101918338|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] [[Category:Sapporo]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]] [[sn:uwa Shrine (Sapporo)]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (札幌市)]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hokkaido]] [[Category:札幌市の神社]] [[Category:札幌市東区の建築物]] [[Category:札幌市東区の歴史]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]]

Torii and Main Hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|43|4|33.5|N|141|21|14.6|E}}}} |location = 1-1-10 Kita 12-jō Higashi, Higashi-ku, Sapporo City, Hokkaido |coordinates = {{coord|43|4|33.5|N|141|21|14.6|E|display=inline,title}} |Rank = Former {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha|qq=}} |deity = {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方神|1=Takeminakata|2=ja|3=タケミナカタ|lt=Takeminakata|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata no Mikoto|qq=}} and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Mikoto]] |consecration_year = March 12, 1882 (Meiji 15) |MainHall = |Festival = September 12 |Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=4|lat_sec=33.5|lon_deg=141|lon_min=21|lon_sec=14.6|label=Suwa Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} }} '''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Higashi-ku]], [[Sapporo City]], [[Hokkaido]]. It is also known as '''Sapporo Suwa Shrine''' (札幌諏訪神社). The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方神|1=Takeminakata|2=ja|3=タケミナカタ|lt=Takeminakata|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata no Mikoto|qq=}} and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Mikoto]]. Although the shrine is located on flat land, its main hall is elevated to protect it from the frequent flooding of the nearby [[:en:Sōsei River|Sōsei River]].『爪句 @ 思い出の都市秘境』No.63 The shrine grounds are home to eight protected trees, including [[:en:Fraxinus mandschurica|Japanese ash]] and [[:en:Ulmus davidiana var. japonica|Japanese elm]], designated by Sapporo City.[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties] == History == * '''1878 (Meiji 11):''' Around 30 settlers from [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], including Ueshima Tadashi, migrated to the area. * '''March 12, 1882 (Meiji 15):''' A branch of the [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in Shinano Province was enshrined here.[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine] * '''February 14, 1898 (Meiji 31):''' Officially recognized as an unranked shrine (無格社). * '''April 19, 1934 (Shōwa 9):''' Elevated to {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha|qq=}} status. That same year, the area was incorporated into Sapporo City, and a new main hall and administration office were constructed. * '''1945 (Shōwa 20):''' An application for promotion to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} was submitted but not realized. * '''1966 (Shōwa 41):''' Construction of a new main hall. * '''1982 (Shōwa 57):''' Centennial celebrations held. * '''1992 (Heisei 4):''' 110th anniversary and roof repairs to the main hall. * '''2002 (Heisei 14):''' 120th anniversary celebrated. * '''2012 (Heisei 24):''' 130th anniversary celebrated. == Features == * '''Preserved Trees:''' Eight trees, including Japanese ash and Japanese elm, designated for conservation by Sapporo City.[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties] * '''Monument:''' The shrine grounds include a monument commemorating its establishment. == Annual Events == * '''September 12:''' The shrine’s annual festival. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine] {{translated page|ja|諏訪神社 (札幌市)|version=101918338|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] [[Category:Sapporo]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]] [[sn:uwa Shrine (Sapporo)]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (札幌市)]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hokkaido]] [[Category:札幌市の神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Higashi-ku, Sapporo]] [[Category:札幌市東区の歴史]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]]

That same year, the area was incorporated into Sapporo City, and a new main hall and administration office were constructed. * '''1945 (Shōwa 20):''' An application for promotion to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} was submitted but not realized. * '''1966 (Shōwa 41):''' Construction of a new main hall. * '''1982 (Shōwa 57):''' Centennial celebrations held. * '''1992 (Heisei 4):''' 110th anniversary and roof repairs to the main hall. * '''2002 (Heisei 14):''' 120th anniversary celebrated. * '''2012 (Heisei 24):''' 130th anniversary celebrated. == Features == * '''Preserved Trees:''' Eight trees, including Japanese ash and Japanese elm, designated for conservation by Sapporo City.[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties] * '''Monument:''' The shrine grounds include a monument commemorating its establishment. == Annual Events == * '''September 12:''' The shrine’s annual festival. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine] {{translated page|ja|諏訪神社 (札幌市)|version=101918338|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] [[Category:Sapporo]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]] [[sn:uwa Shrine (Sapporo)]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (札幌市)]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Shrines in Sapporo]] [[Category:Buildings in Higashi-ku, Sapporo]] [[Category:札幌市東区の歴史]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] {{Commons category|Suwa-jinja (Sapporo)}}

That same year, the area was incorporated into Sapporo City, and a new main hall and administration office were constructed. * '''1945 (Shōwa 20):''' An application for promotion to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} was submitted but not realized. * '''1966 (Shōwa 41):''' Construction of a new main hall. * '''1982 (Shōwa 57):''' Centennial celebrations held. * '''1992 (Heisei 4):''' 110th anniversary and roof repairs to the main hall. * '''2002 (Heisei 14):''' 120th anniversary celebrated. * '''2012 (Heisei 24):''' 130th anniversary celebrated. == Features == * '''Preserved Trees:''' Eight trees, including Japanese ash and Japanese elm, designated for conservation by Sapporo City.[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties] * '''Monument:''' The shrine grounds include a monument commemorating its establishment. == Annual Events == * '''September 12:''' The shrine’s annual festival. == References == == External Links == * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine] {{translated page|ja|諏訪神社 (札幌市)|version=101918338|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sapporo]] [[Category:Unranked shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Suwa Shrine (Sapporo)]] [[sn:uwa Shrine (Sapporo)]] [[ja:諏訪神社 (札幌市)]] [[Category:Son-sha in Hokkaido]] [[Category:Shrines in Sapporo]] [[Category:Buildings in Higashi-ku, Sapporo]] [[Category:札幌市東区の歴史]] {{Commons category|Suwa-jinja (Sapporo)}}

'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Higashi-ku|Higashi-ku]], [[Sapporo City]], [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]]. It is also known as '''Sapporo Suwa Shrine''' (札幌諏訪神社). The shrine enshrines [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Mikoto]].
Although the shrine is located on flat land, its main hall is elevated to protect it from the frequent flooding of the nearby [[:en:Sōsei River|Sōsei River]].<ref name="tsumeku">『爪句 @ 思い出の都市秘境』No.63</ref> The shrine grounds are home to eight protected trees, including [[:en:Fraxinus mandschurica|Japanese ash]] and [[:en:Ulmus davidiana var. japonica|Japanese elm]], designated by Sapporo City.<ref>[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties]</ref>
== History == * '''1878 (Meiji 11):''' Around 30 settlers from [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], including Ueshima Tadashi, migrated to the area.<ref name="tsumeku" /> * '''March 12, 1882 (Meiji 15):''' A branch of the [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in Shinano Province was enshrined here.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine]</ref> * '''February 14, 1898 (Meiji 31):''' Officially recognized as an unranked shrine (無格社).<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''April 19, 1934 (Shōwa 9):''' Elevated to {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} status. That same year, the area was incorporated into Sapporo City, and a new main hall and administration office were constructed.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''1945 (Shōwa 20):''' An application for promotion to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} was submitted but not realized.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''1966 (Shōwa 41):''' Construction of a new main hall.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''1982 (Shōwa 57):''' Centennial celebrations held.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''1992 (Heisei 4):''' 110th anniversary and roof repairs to the main hall.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''2002 (Heisei 14):''' 120th anniversary celebrated.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''2012 (Heisei 24):''' 130th anniversary celebrated.<ref name="jinjacho" />
== Features == * '''Preserved Trees:''' Eight trees, including Japanese ash and Japanese elm, designated for conservation by Sapporo City.<ref>[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties]</ref> * '''Monument:''' The shrine grounds include a monument commemorating its establishment.
== Annual Events == * '''September 12:''' The shrine’s annual festival.
== References == <references /> == External Links == * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine]
