{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q20045170]] Wikidata name: Suwa-jinja (sapporo) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Suwa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Suwa Shrine of Sapporo.JPG |location = 1-1-10 Kita 12-jō Higashi, Higashi-ku, Sapporo City, Hokkaido |coordinates = {{Coord|43.07597222222222|N|141.35405555555556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Rank = Former {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |deity = {{ill|Takeminakata|qid=Q9150391}} |consecration_year = March 12, 1882 (Meiji 15) |MainHall = |Festival = September 12 |Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=4|lat_sec=33.5|lon_deg=141|lon_min=21|lon_sec=14.6|label=Suwa Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} |location = {{ill|Sapporo|qid=Q37951}} }}
'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Higashi-ku|Higashi-ku]], [[Sapporo City]], [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]]. It is also known as '''Sapporo Suwa Shrine''' (札幌諏訪神社). The shrine enshrines [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Mikoto]].
Although the shrine is located on flat land, its main hall is elevated to protect it from the frequent flooding of the nearby [[:en:Sōsei River|Sōsei River]].<ref name="tsumeku">『爪句 @ 思い出の都市秘境』No.63</ref> The shrine grounds are home to eight protected trees, including [[:en:Fraxinus mandschurica|Japanese ash]] and [[:en:Ulmus davidiana var. japonica|Japanese elm]], designated by Sapporo City.<ref>[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties]</ref>
== History == * '''1878 (Meiji 11):''' Around 30 settlers from [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], including Ueshima Tadashi, migrated to the area.<ref name="tsumeku" /> * '''March 12, 1882 (Meiji 15):''' A branch of the [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in Shinano Province was enshrined here.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine]</ref> * '''February 14, 1898 (Meiji 31):''' Officially recognized as an unranked shrine (無格社).<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''April 19, 1934 (Shōwa 9):''' Elevated to {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} status. That same year, the area was incorporated into Sapporo City, and a new main hall and administration office were constructed.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''1945 (Shōwa 20):''' An application for promotion to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} was submitted but not realized.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''1966 (Shōwa 41):''' Construction of a new main hall.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''1982 (Shōwa 57):''' Centennial celebrations held.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''1992 (Heisei 4):''' 110th anniversary and roof repairs to the main hall.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''2002 (Heisei 14):''' 120th anniversary celebrated.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''2012 (Heisei 24):''' 130th anniversary celebrated.<ref name="jinjacho" />
== Features == * '''Preserved Trees:''' Eight trees, including Japanese ash and Japanese elm, designated for conservation by Sapporo City.<ref>[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties]</ref> * '''Monument:''' The shrine grounds include a monument commemorating its establishment.
== Annual Events == * '''September 12:''' The shrine’s annual festival.
== References == <references /> == External Links == * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine]

== History == * '''1878 (Meiji 11):''' Around 30 settlers from [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], including Ueshima Tadashi, migrated to the area.<ref name="tsumeku" /> * '''March 12, 1882 (Meiji 15):''' A branch of the [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in Shinano Province was enshrined here.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine]</ref> * '''February 14, 1898 (Meiji 31):''' Officially recognized as an unranked shrine (無格社).<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''April 19, 1934 (Shōwa 9):''' Elevated to {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} status. That same year, the area was incorporated into Sapporo City, and a new main hall and administration office were constructed.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''1945 (Shōwa 20):''' An application for promotion to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} was submitted but not realized.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''1966 (Shōwa 41):''' Construction of a new main hall.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''1982 (Shōwa 57):''' Centennial celebrations held.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''1992 (Heisei 4):''' 110th anniversary and roof repairs to the main hall.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''2002 (Heisei 14):''' 120th anniversary celebrated.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''2012 (Heisei 24):''' 130th anniversary celebrated.<ref name="jinjacho" />
== Features == * '''Preserved Trees:''' Eight trees, including Japanese ash and Japanese elm, designated for conservation by Sapporo City.<ref>[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties]</ref> * '''Monument:''' The shrine grounds include a monument commemorating its establishment.

'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Higashi-ku|Higashi-ku]], [[Sapporo City]], [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]]. It is also known as '''Sapporo Suwa Shrine''' (札幌諏訪神社). The shrine enshrines [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Mikoto]].
Although the shrine is located on flat land, its main hall is elevated to protect it from the frequent flooding of the nearby [[:en:Sōsei River|Sōsei River]].<ref name="tsumeku">『爪句 @ 思い出の都市秘境』No.63</ref> The shrine grounds are home to eight protected trees, including [[:en:Fraxinus mandschurica|Japanese ash]] and [[:en:Ulmus davidiana var. japonica|Japanese elm]], designated by Sapporo City.<ref>[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties]</ref>
== History == * '''1878 (Meiji 11):''' Around 30 settlers from [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], including Ueshima Tadashi, migrated to the area.<ref name="tsumeku" /> * '''March 12, 1882 (Meiji 15):''' A branch of the [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in Shinano Province was enshrined here.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine]</ref> * '''February 14, 1898 (Meiji 31):''' Officially recognized as an unranked shrine (無格社).<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''April 19, 1934 (Shōwa 9):''' Elevated to {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} status. That same year, the area was incorporated into Sapporo City, and a new main hall and administration office were constructed.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''1945 (Shōwa 20):''' An application for promotion to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} was submitted but not realized.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''1966 (Shōwa 41):''' Construction of a new main hall.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''1982 (Shōwa 57):''' Centennial celebrations held.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''1992 (Heisei 4):''' 110th anniversary and roof repairs to the main hall.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''2002 (Heisei 14):''' 120th anniversary celebrated.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''2012 (Heisei 24):''' 130th anniversary celebrated.<ref name="jinjacho" />
== Features == * '''Preserved Trees:''' Eight trees, including Japanese ash and Japanese elm, designated for conservation by Sapporo City.<ref>[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties]</ref> * '''Monument:''' The shrine grounds include a monument commemorating its establishment.
== Annual Events == * '''September 12:''' The shrine’s annual festival.
== References == <references /> == External Links == * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Suwa-jinja (Sapporo)]][[ja:諏訪神社 (札幌市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q20045170]] Wikidata name: Suwa-jinja (sapporo) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Suwa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Suwa Shrine of Sapporo.JPG |location = 1-1-10 Kita 12-jō Higashi, Higashi-ku, Sapporo City, Hokkaido |coordinates = {{Coord|43.07597222222222|N|141.35405555555556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Rank = Former {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |deity = {{ill|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391}} |consecration_year = March 12, 1882 (Meiji 15) |MainHall = |Festival = September 12 |Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=4|lat_sec=33.5|lon_deg=141|lon_min=21|lon_sec=14.6|label=Suwa Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} |location = {{ill|Sapporo|WD=Q37951}} }}
'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Higashi-ku|Higashi-ku]], [[Sapporo City]], [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]]. It is also known as '''Sapporo Suwa Shrine''' (札幌諏訪神社). The shrine enshrines [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Mikoto]].
Although the shrine is located on flat land, its main hall is elevated to protect it from the frequent flooding of the nearby [[:en:Sōsei River|Sōsei River]].<ref name="tsumeku">『爪句 @ 思い出の都市秘境』No.63</ref> The shrine grounds are home to eight protected trees, including [[:en:Fraxinus mandschurica|Japanese ash]] and [[:en:Ulmus davidiana var. japonica|Japanese elm]], designated by Sapporo City.<ref>[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties]</ref>
== History == * '''1878 (Meiji 11):''' Around 30 settlers from [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], including Ueshima Tadashi, migrated to the area.<ref name="tsumeku" /> * '''March 12, 1882 (Meiji 15):''' A branch of the [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in Shinano Province was enshrined here.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine]</ref> * '''February 14, 1898 (Meiji 31):''' Officially recognized as an unranked shrine (無格社).<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''April 19, 1934 (Shōwa 9):''' Elevated to {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} status. That same year, the area was incorporated into Sapporo City, and a new main hall and administration office were constructed.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''1945 (Shōwa 20):''' An application for promotion to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} was submitted but not realized.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''1966 (Shōwa 41):''' Construction of a new main hall.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''1982 (Shōwa 57):''' Centennial celebrations held.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''1992 (Heisei 4):''' 110th anniversary and roof repairs to the main hall.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''2002 (Heisei 14):''' 120th anniversary celebrated.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''2012 (Heisei 24):''' 130th anniversary celebrated.<ref name="jinjacho" />
== Features == * '''Preserved Trees:''' Eight trees, including Japanese ash and Japanese elm, designated for conservation by Sapporo City.<ref>[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties]</ref> * '''Monument:''' The shrine grounds include a monument commemorating its establishment.
== Annual Events == * '''September 12:''' The shrine’s annual festival.
== References == <references /> == External Links == * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine]

'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Higashi-ku|Higashi-ku]], [[Sapporo City]], [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]]. It is also known as '''Sapporo Suwa Shrine''' (札幌諏訪神社). The shrine enshrines [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Mikoto]].
Although the shrine is located on flat land, its main hall is elevated to protect it from the frequent flooding of the nearby [[:en:Sōsei River|Sōsei River]].<ref name="tsumeku">『爪句 @ 思い出の都市秘境』No.63</ref> The shrine grounds are home to eight protected trees, including [[:en:Fraxinus mandschurica|Japanese ash]] and [[:en:Ulmus davidiana var. japonica|Japanese elm]], designated by Sapporo City.<ref>[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties]</ref>
== History == * '''1878 (Meiji 11):''' Around 30 settlers from [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], including Ueshima Tadashi, migrated to the area.<ref name="tsumeku" /> * '''March 12, 1882 (Meiji 15):''' A branch of the [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in Shinano Province was enshrined here.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine]</ref> * '''February 14, 1898 (Meiji 31):''' Officially recognized as an unranked shrine (無格社).<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''April 19, 1934 (Shōwa 9):''' Elevated to {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} status. That same year, the area was incorporated into Sapporo City, and a new main hall and administration office were constructed.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''1945 (Shōwa 20):''' An application for promotion to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} was submitted but not realized.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''1966 (Shōwa 41):''' Construction of a new main hall.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''1982 (Shōwa 57):''' Centennial celebrations held.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''1992 (Heisei 4):''' 110th anniversary and roof repairs to the main hall.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''2002 (Heisei 14):''' 120th anniversary celebrated.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''2012 (Heisei 24):''' 130th anniversary celebrated.<ref name="jinjacho" />
== Features == * '''Preserved Trees:''' Eight trees, including Japanese ash and Japanese elm, designated for conservation by Sapporo City.<ref>[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties]</ref> * '''Monument:''' The shrine grounds include a monument commemorating its establishment.
== Annual Events == * '''September 12:''' The shrine’s annual festival.
== References == <references /> == External Links == * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine]
{{translated page|ja|諏訪神社 (札幌市)|version=101918338|comment=Imported full ja history}}

'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Higashi-ku|Higashi-ku]], [[Sapporo City]], [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]]. It is also known as '''Sapporo Suwa Shrine''' (札幌諏訪神社). The shrine enshrines [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Mikoto]].
Although the shrine is located on flat land, its main hall is elevated to protect it from the frequent flooding of the nearby [[:en:Sōsei River|Sōsei River]].<ref name="tsumeku">『爪句 @ 思い出の都市秘境』No.63</ref> The shrine grounds are home to eight protected trees, including [[:en:Fraxinus mandschurica|Japanese ash]] and [[:en:Ulmus davidiana var. japonica|Japanese elm]], designated by Sapporo City.<ref>[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties]</ref>
== History == * '''1878 (Meiji 11):''' Around 30 settlers from [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], including Ueshima Tadashi, migrated to the area.<ref name="tsumeku" /> * '''March 12, 1882 (Meiji 15):''' A branch of the [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in Shinano Province was enshrined here.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine]</ref> * '''February 14, 1898 (Meiji 31):''' Officially recognized as an unranked shrine (無格社).<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''April 19, 1934 (Shōwa 9):''' Elevated to {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} status. That same year, the area was incorporated into Sapporo City, and a new main hall and administration office were constructed.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''1945 (Shōwa 20):''' An application for promotion to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} was submitted but not realized.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''1966 (Shōwa 41):''' Construction of a new main hall.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''1982 (Shōwa 57):''' Centennial celebrations held.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''1992 (Heisei 4):''' 110th anniversary and roof repairs to the main hall.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''2002 (Heisei 14):''' 120th anniversary celebrated.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''2012 (Heisei 24):''' 130th anniversary celebrated.<ref name="jinjacho" />
== Features == * '''Preserved Trees:''' Eight trees, including Japanese ash and Japanese elm, designated for conservation by Sapporo City.<ref>[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties]</ref> * '''Monument:''' The shrine grounds include a monument commemorating its establishment.
== Annual Events == * '''September 12:''' The shrine’s annual festival.
== References == <references /> == External Links == * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine]
{{translated page|ja|諏訪神社 (札幌市)|version=101918338|comment=Imported full ja history}}

'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Higashi-ku|Higashi-ku]], [[Sapporo City]], [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]]. It is also known as '''Sapporo Suwa Shrine''' (札幌諏訪神社). The shrine enshrines [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Mikoto]].
Although the shrine is located on flat land, its main hall is elevated to protect it from the frequent flooding of the nearby [[:en:Sōsei River|Sōsei River]].<ref name="tsumeku">『爪句 @ 思い出の都市秘境』No.63</ref> The shrine grounds are home to eight protected trees, including [[:en:Fraxinus mandschurica|Japanese ash]] and [[:en:Ulmus davidiana var. japonica|Japanese elm]], designated by Sapporo City.<ref>[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties]</ref>
== History == * '''1878 (Meiji 11):''' Around 30 settlers from [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], including Ueshima Tadashi, migrated to the area.<ref name="tsumeku" /> * '''March 12, 1882 (Meiji 15):''' A branch of the [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] in Shinano Province was enshrined here.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine]</ref> * '''February 14, 1898 (Meiji 31):''' Officially recognized as an unranked shrine (無格社).<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''April 19, 1934 (Shōwa 9):''' Elevated to {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} status. That same year, the area was incorporated into Sapporo City, and a new main hall and administration office were constructed.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''1945 (Shōwa 20):''' An application for promotion to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} was submitted but not realized.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''1966 (Shōwa 41):''' Construction of a new main hall.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''1982 (Shōwa 57):''' Centennial celebrations held.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''1992 (Heisei 4):''' 110th anniversary and roof repairs to the main hall.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''2002 (Heisei 14):''' 120th anniversary celebrated.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''2012 (Heisei 24):''' 130th anniversary celebrated.<ref name="jinjacho" />
== Features == * '''Preserved Trees:''' Eight trees, including Japanese ash and Japanese elm, designated for conservation by Sapporo City.<ref>[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties]</ref> * '''Monument:''' The shrine grounds include a monument commemorating its establishment.
== Annual Events == * '''September 12:''' The shrine’s annual festival.
== References == <references /> == External Links == * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine]
{{translated page|ja|諏訪神社 (札幌市)|version=101918338|comment=Imported full ja history}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Suwa-jinja (Sapporo)]][[ja:諏訪神社 (札幌市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Suwa-jinja (Sapporo)]][[ja:諏訪神社 (札幌市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Suwa-jinja (Sapporo)]][[ja:諏訪神社 (札幌市)]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Suwa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Suwa Shrine of Sapporo.JPG |location = 1-1-10 Kita 12-jō Higashi, Higashi-ku, Sapporo City, Hokkaido |coordinates = {{Coord|43.07597222222222|N|141.35405555555556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Rank = Former {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |deity = {{ill|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391}} |consecration_year = March 12, 1882 (Meiji 15) |MainHall = |Festival = September 12 |Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=4|lat_sec=33.5|lon_deg=141|lon_min=21|lon_sec=14.6|label=Suwa Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} |location = {{ill|Sapporo|WD=Q37951}} }}
'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Higashi-ku|en|Higashi-ku|WD=Q904341|lt=Higashi-ku}}, [[Sapporo City]], [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]]. It is also known as '''Sapporo Suwa Shrine''' (札幌諏訪神社). The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|en|Yasakatome|WD=Q9150392|lt=Yasakatome no Mikoto}}.
Although the shrine is located on flat land, its main hall is elevated to protect it from the frequent flooding of the nearby {{ill|Sōsei River|en|Sōsei River|WD=Q11398074|lt=Sōsei River}}.<ref name="tsumeku">『爪句 @ 思い出の都市秘境』No.63</ref> The shrine grounds are home to eight protected trees, including {{ill|Fraxinus mandschurica|en|Fraxinus mandschurica|WD=Q1357301|lt=Japanese ash}} and {{ill|Ulmus davidiana var. japonica|en|Ulmus davidiana var. japonica|WD=Q844010|lt=Japanese elm}}, designated by Sapporo City.<ref>[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties]</ref>
== History == * '''1878 (Meiji 11):''' Around 30 settlers from {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}, including Ueshima Tadashi, migrated to the area.<ref name="tsumeku" /> * '''March 12, 1882 (Meiji 15):''' A branch of the {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha}} in Shinano Province was enshrined here.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine]</ref> * '''February 14, 1898 (Meiji 31):''' Officially recognized as an unranked shrine (無格社).<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''April 19, 1934 (Shōwa 9):''' Elevated to {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} status. That same year, the area was incorporated into Sapporo City, and a new main hall and administration office were constructed.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''1945 (Shōwa 20):''' An application for promotion to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} was submitted but not realized.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''1966 (Shōwa 41):''' Construction of a new main hall.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''1982 (Shōwa 57):''' Centennial celebrations held.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''1992 (Heisei 4):''' 110th anniversary and roof repairs to the main hall.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''2002 (Heisei 14):''' 120th anniversary celebrated.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''2012 (Heisei 24):''' 130th anniversary celebrated.<ref name="jinjacho" />
== Features == * '''Preserved Trees:''' Eight trees, including Japanese ash and Japanese elm, designated for conservation by Sapporo City.<ref>[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties]</ref> * '''Monument:''' The shrine grounds include a monument commemorating its establishment.
== Annual Events == * '''September 12:''' The shrine’s annual festival.
== References == <references /> == External Links == * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine]
{{translated page|ja|諏訪神社 (札幌市)|version=101918338|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Suwa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Suwa Shrine of Sapporo.JPG |location = 1-1-10 Kita 12-jō Higashi, Higashi-ku, Sapporo City, Hokkaido |coordinates = {{Coord|43.07597222222222|N|141.35405555555556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Rank = Former {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |deity = {{ill|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|1=Takeminakata}} |consecration_year = March 12, 1882 (Meiji 15) |MainHall = |Festival = September 12 |Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=4|lat_sec=33.5|lon_deg=141|lon_min=21|lon_sec=14.6|label=Suwa Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} |location = {{ill|Sapporo|WD=Q37951|1=Sapporo}} }}
'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Higashi-ku|en|Higashi-ku|WD=Q904341|lt=Higashi-ku|1=Higashi-ku}}, [[Sapporo City]], [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]]. It is also known as '''Sapporo Suwa Shrine''' (札幌諏訪神社). The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|1=Takeminakata}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|en|Yasakatome|WD=Q9150392|lt=Yasakatome no Mikoto|1=Yasakatome}}.
Although the shrine is located on flat land, its main hall is elevated to protect it from the frequent flooding of the nearby {{ill|Sōsei River|en|Sōsei River|WD=Q11398074|lt=Sōsei River|1=Sōsei River}}.<ref name="tsumeku">『爪句 @ 思い出の都市秘境』No.63</ref> The shrine grounds are home to eight protected trees, including {{ill|Fraxinus mandschurica|en|Fraxinus mandschurica|WD=Q1357301|lt=Japanese ash|1=Fraxinus mandschurica}} and {{ill|Ulmus davidiana var. japonica|en|Ulmus davidiana var. japonica|WD=Q844010|lt=Japanese elm|1=Ulmus davidiana var. japonica}}, designated by Sapporo City.<ref>[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties]</ref>
== History == * '''1878 (Meiji 11):''' Around 30 settlers from {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}, including Ueshima Tadashi, migrated to the area.<ref name="tsumeku" /> * '''March 12, 1882 (Meiji 15):''' A branch of the {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha|1=Suwa-taisha}} in Shinano Province was enshrined here.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine]</ref> * '''February 14, 1898 (Meiji 31):''' Officially recognized as an unranked shrine (無格社).<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''April 19, 1934 (Shōwa 9):''' Elevated to {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} status. That same year, the area was incorporated into Sapporo City, and a new main hall and administration office were constructed.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''1945 (Shōwa 20):''' An application for promotion to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} was submitted but not realized.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''1966 (Shōwa 41):''' Construction of a new main hall.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''1982 (Shōwa 57):''' Centennial celebrations held.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''1992 (Heisei 4):''' 110th anniversary and roof repairs to the main hall.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''2002 (Heisei 14):''' 120th anniversary celebrated.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''2012 (Heisei 24):''' 130th anniversary celebrated.<ref name="jinjacho" />
== Features == * '''Preserved Trees:''' Eight trees, including Japanese ash and Japanese elm, designated for conservation by Sapporo City.<ref>[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties]</ref> * '''Monument:''' The shrine grounds include a monument commemorating its establishment.
== Annual Events == * '''September 12:''' The shrine’s annual festival.
== References == <references /> == External Links == * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Suwa-jinja (Sapporo)]][[ja:諏訪神社 (札幌市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Suwa-jinja (Sapporo)]][[ja:諏訪神社 (札幌市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Suwa-jinja (Sapporo)]][[ja:諏訪神社 (札幌市)]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Suwa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Suwa Shrine of Sapporo.JPG |location = 1-1-10 Kita 12-jō Higashi, Higashi-ku, Sapporo City, Hokkaido |coordinates = {{Coord|43.07597222222222|N|141.35405555555556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Rank = Former {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |deity = {{ill|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|1=Takeminakata}} |consecration_year = March 12, 1882 (Meiji 15) |MainHall = |Festival = September 12 |Map = {{location map|Japan Hokkaido|lat_deg=43|lat_min=4|lat_sec=33.5|lon_deg=141|lon_min=21|lon_sec=14.6|label=Suwa Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|AlternativeMap=Hokkaidō géolocalisation 000.svg}} |location = {{ill|Sapporo|WD=Q37951|1=Sapporo}} }}
'''Suwa Shrine''' (諏訪神社) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Higashi-ku|en|Higashi-ku|WD=Q904341|lt=Higashi-ku|1=Higashi-ku}}, [[Sapporo City]], [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaidō]]. It is also known as '''Sapporo Suwa Shrine''' (札幌諏訪神社). The shrine enshrines {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|1=Takeminakata}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|en|Yasakatome|WD=Q9150392|lt=Yasakatome no Mikoto|1=Yasakatome}}.
Although the shrine is located on flat land, its main hall is elevated to protect it from the frequent flooding of the nearby {{ill|Sōsei River|en|Sōsei River|WD=Q11398074|lt=Sōsei River|1=Sōsei River}}.<ref name="tsumeku">『爪句 @ 思い出の都市秘境』No.63</ref> The shrine grounds are home to eight protected trees, including {{ill|Fraxinus mandschurica|en|Fraxinus mandschurica|WD=Q1357301|lt=Japanese ash|1=Fraxinus mandschurica}} and {{ill|Ulmus davidiana var. japonica|en|Ulmus davidiana var. japonica|WD=Q844010|lt=Japanese elm|1=Ulmus davidiana var. japonica}}, designated by Sapporo City.<ref>[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties]</ref>
== History == * '''1878 (Meiji 11):''' Around 30 settlers from {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}, including Ueshima Tadashi, migrated to the area.<ref name="tsumeku" /> * '''March 12, 1882 (Meiji 15):''' A branch of the {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha|1=Suwa-taisha}} in Shinano Province was enshrined here.<ref name="jinjacho">[http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine]</ref> * '''February 14, 1898 (Meiji 31):''' Officially recognized as an unranked shrine (無格社).<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''April 19, 1934 (Shōwa 9):''' Elevated to {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} status. That same year, the area was incorporated into Sapporo City, and a new main hall and administration office were constructed.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''1945 (Shōwa 20):''' An application for promotion to {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} was submitted but not realized.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''1966 (Shōwa 41):''' Construction of a new main hall.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''1982 (Shōwa 57):''' Centennial celebrations held.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''1992 (Heisei 4):''' 110th anniversary and roof repairs to the main hall.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''2002 (Heisei 14):''' 120th anniversary celebrated.<ref name="jinjacho" /> * '''2012 (Heisei 24):''' 130th anniversary celebrated.<ref name="jinjacho" />
== Features == * '''Preserved Trees:''' Eight trees, including Japanese ash and Japanese elm, designated for conservation by Sapporo City.<ref>[http://www.city.sapporo.jp/higashi/about/ins_culturer.html Sapporo City Higashi Ward - Cultural Properties]</ref> * '''Monument:''' The shrine grounds include a monument commemorating its establishment.
== Annual Events == * '''September 12:''' The shrine’s annual festival.
== References == <references /> == External Links == * [http://www.hokkaidojinjacho.jp/data/01/01004.html Hokkaido Shrine Association - Suwa Shrine]
{{translated page|ja|諏訪神社 (札幌市)|version=101918338|comment=Imported full ja history}}
